Ad Text:
FinXL Professional Services is a leading IT consultancy that partners with clients to bridge the gap between business needs and technology capabilities. Since 2003, we've been delivering high-value ICT services across government, finance, telecommunications, and enterprise sectors.

FinXL is seeking a Senior Data Integration Engineer - Level 5 to join our consulting team and be deployed with a leading organisation. The Senior Data Integration Engineer - Level 5 will lead the data integration piece to design & deliver event-driven architectures.

Key Skills & Experience

Experience in Senior Data Integration Engineering - Level 5
Experience driving the evolution of data integration capabilities to enable event-driven data architectures.
Experience in Real-Time Architecture: Designing and deploying event-driven integration patterns to enable near-instantaneous data responses across platforms.
Experience in Streaming & Messaging: Serving as the subject matter expert for Confluent Kafka, managing complex message schemas and ensuring seamless stream processing.
Experience in Technical Leadership: Acting as a Level 5 senior contributor, providing architectural guidance, conducting deep-dive code reviews and mentoring junior engineers. You will be responsible for authoring options papers to the Architecture Board and articulating the benefits to senior non-technical sponsors.
Experience in Documentation & Governance: Maintaining technical documentation & architectural diagrams within Confluence to ensure transparency and knowledge sharing
Experience in Confluence to document technical designs and operational playbooks.
Financial services industry experience would be an advantage

Next Steps

If you would like to apply to this fantastic opportunity please apply online or call Jackie Gibbons on 0432 268 298

Code: S12

Join an AI powerhouse that is going through a rapid expansion. You will help to build autonomous AI workers. This is a permanent, full-time position. This is a hybrid position and candidates must be Sydney based. 1 day per week/fortnight. 

The Opportunity

As a Staff AI Engineer (Data Science), you’ll play a key role in building and improving the next generation of large language model (LLM)‑powered solutions. You’ll work across varied use cases, apply advanced statistical and machine learning techniques, and contribute directly to products used by major global enterprises.
You’ll thrive here if you love solving complex challenges, collaborating with talented peers, and turning cutting‑edge research into practical impact.

What you’ll do:

Develop and deploy machine learning models that shape products and user experience
Analyse large datasets to identify patterns, insights, and opportunities
Improve LLM output quality across diverse use cases
Plan and implement MLOps workflows, including benchmarking, QA, and compliance
Influence long‑term data strategy, governance, and ethical AI practices
Partner closely with engineers, product teams, and stakeholders
Mentor junior data scientists and contribute to a high‑performance team culture
Skills Required:

4–12 years’ experience as a Data Scientist or AI/ML Engineer
A Master’s or Doctorate in Data Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, or a related discipline (preferred but not essential)
Strong experience working with structured and unstructured data
Advanced SQL and Python experience
Experience applying statistical techniques and presenting results
Commercial experience with NLP techniques (LSTM/CNN/Transformers, supervised NLP, topic modelling)
Experience with GPT‑based frameworks (LangChain, LlamaIndex, RAG, multi‑agent frameworks)
Ability to interpret and apply the latest LLM research
Ability to thrive in a fast‑paced, agile environment
If interested, APPLY NOW for more details!

We are one of Australia's fastest growing companies. We design, build and maintain world-class digital infrastructure, encompassing fibre networks, communication platforms, residential internet and voice services.

We believe our people are vital to our success. We strive to create an environment that appeals to the very best people in the industry, providing them with an exciting, market leading and satisfying workplace, and rewarding their performance.




About the Role:

The Access Network Automation Engineer, designs, develops, and maintains automation solutions that integrate FTTP and network systems into our OSS. This role improves network efficiency, consistency, and reliability through scripting, automation, and data integration across modern and legacy networks, working closely with engineering, operations, and OSS teams to streamline provisioning, configuration, testing, and data management.

Responsibilities include but not limited to:

Design, develop, and maintain software solutions to automate FTTP network workflows
Integrate network elements and platforms into OSS using APIs, scripts, and data interfaces
Develop and maintain scripts for network configuration, validation, deployment, and auditing
Extract, transform, and manage network data for operational use, reporting, and troubleshooting
Build and execute automated test frameworks for scripts, configurations, and integrations
Support integration with both modern and legacy network equipment across various vendor interfaces
Troubleshoot automation, integration, and data issues across OSS and network systems
Collaborate with engineers, operations teams, and developers to meet operational requirements
Contribute to documentation, coding standards, and best practices in network automation
Continuously identify opportunities to improve efficiency, reliability, and scalability of automation processes

About you:

The ideal person for this role will have/be:

Passionate about software development and network automation
Focused and detail-oriented, especially with configuration, data, and automation logic
Curious and adaptable, eager to learn new technologies, tools, and network platforms
Comfortable working in complex environments with both modern and legacy systems
Highly collaborative, working effectively with cross-functional technical teams
Self-motivated, able to manage priorities and work independently
Clear and confident communicator, able to explain technical concepts to all stakeholders

Essential Experience:

Degree in Computer Systems Design, Computer Science, or a related field
Proven experience as a Software Developer, Automation Engineer, or similar role in a networked/infrastructure environment
Strong scripting and development skills (e.g., Python, Bash, or similar)
Experience integrating systems using APIs, data formats (JSON, XML), and automation frameworks
Skilled in extracting, transforming, and managing data from multiple systems
Solid understanding of networking concepts, ideally including FTTP, access networks, or service provider environments
Experience testing and validating scripts and automation workflows
Experience working with network devices is desirable
Familiarity with Postman or other API testing tools is highly desirable

We’re looking for an experienced Business Analyst with a strong background in AI-enabled solutions to support the design and delivery of intelligent, automation-driven capabilities.

You’ll work across business and technical teams to translate complex problems into AI-enabled workflows, decision-support tools and automation solutions, ensuring outcomes are practical, scalable and aligned to governance requirements.

This role will suit someone who has moved beyond traditional BA work and has hands-on experience delivering AI or intelligent automation initiatives from discovery through to production.

Key responsibilities:

Work with stakeholders to understand business problems and identify where AI or automation can add value
Translate requirements into clear user stories and acceptance criteria for delivery teams
Map and design end-to-end processes, including where AI, rules or manual steps are used
Collaborate with engineers and data teams to support delivery of AI-enabled solutions
Help define how solutions are tested, measured and improved over time
Ensure solutions meet governance, risk and compliance requirements
Run workshops and communicate effectively with both business and technical stakeholders
Support rollout and adoption of new AI-enabled tools and processes

The ideal candidate will have the following experience:

3–5+ years’ experience as a Business Analyst in agile environments
Experience delivering AI, ML or intelligent automation solutions
Strong experience writing user stories and acceptance criteria for complex solutions
Ability to work closely with technical teams (developers, data, engineering, architecture)

This is not a standard support engineering role.
  
You’ll be working on complex, custom-built mechatronic and motion systems deployed globally in high-profile, technically demanding environments. These are precision machines combining mechanical engineering, automation, controls and motion technology. When something needs diagnosing, validating or improving, you’re the person stepping in.
  
The role sits within a high-performing Service and Support team and blends mechanical depth with applied PLC and motion troubleshooting. It is hands-on, analytical and collaborative.
  
The Role
You’ll sit within a high-performing Service & Support team, providing mechanical and controls engineering expertise across new and existing installations.
  
Day to day, you will:

• Design and modify mechanical and mechatronic components
• Maintain SolidWorks models and technical drawings
• Perform core engineering calculations and validation
• Troubleshoot PLC systems including Beckhoff and Allen Bradley
• Diagnose servo, hydraulic and electric motion issues
• Conduct root cause analysis on complex faults
• Produce clear technical documentation and service notes
• Support global clients remotely and attend site when required
• Collaborate closely with engineers, designers and technicians
  
What You’ll Bring

• Degree in Mechatronics, Mechanical Engineering or similar
• Strong mechanical fundamentals with hands-on troubleshooting experience
• SolidWorks capability
• PLC exposure ideally Beckhoff and/or Allen Bradley
• Exposure to custom machinery or safety-critical systems is advantageous
• Australian Citizen or PR
  
This role is not one you want to miss, so if you want to hear more please don't hesitate to reach out!
nat@justdigitalpeople.com.au

The Strategy and Performance Division promotes the value of education in South Australia and plays a key role in improving the public education system. It leads system-wide strategy, manages strategic risks, provides data-driven insights, and supports schools and preschools to succeed and strengthen their standing within their communities.

The Data Operations and Service directorate is responsible for ingesting, storing, organising, and managing data held in the Department’s data warehouses/repositories. It is the engine room of Strategy and Performance, ensuring that data is clean, high quality, usable and available. The team is also responsible for liaising with customers to assess and prioritise their requirements, and leading data governance operations for the Department. Data Operations and Service manages the full range of data life cycle functions, including business analysis, data engineering, product development, and testing.

Please refer to position descriptions for AS03 - AS08 role details

AS03-AS06:  A tertiary qualification in IT, Computer Science, Data Science or related field or equivalent experience.

Certifications in Microsoft Azure, SQL, Python, Databricks, or other cloud data tools/providers.
